rulings.Case Title: GEORGE LOEW ET AL VS. ALICE M. TRAEG
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Confirm Contractual Arbitrtation Award And For Entry Of Judgment Thereon
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 29, 2021 line 1. PETITIONER'S NOTICE OF HEARING ON PETITION TO CONFIRM CONTRACTUAL ARBITRATION AWARD AND FOR ENTRY OF JUDGMENT THEREON is OFF CALENDAR. Amended Petition on file. Petitioner to set a hearing based on the Amended Petition to Confirm Arbitration. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: REZA R. DIBADJ VS. AMERICAN CONTRACTORS INDEMNITY COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 29, 2021 line 3. DEFENDANT AMERICAN CONTRACTORS INDEMNITY COMPANY'S DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is OFF CALENDAR. Notice of Demurrer filed on June 21, 2021 is not supported by motion or points and authorities. Prior demurrer was taken off calendar on June 15, 2021 and no order of the Court was obtained to allow the use of previously filed briefs in support of and in opposition to the demurrer in conjunction with the Court ordered response. No courtesy copies of any filings were provided to the Court, so even if the Court were to overlook parties' decision to disregard judge Ulmer's order to file a response by July 9, 2021 after engaging in meet and confer efforts, it is not clear what briefs the parties indented to be considered by the Court. June 21, 2021 Notice of Demurrer and Declaration of counsel do not specifically provide such information (title of the filing to be considered, date of the filing, etc). =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL CRITTENDON VS. ERICK R OSORIO
rulings.Calendar Matter: HEARING ON CLAIM OF RIGHT TO POSSESSION
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 29, 2021 line 4. DEFENDANT ESTRELLA MENDOZA'S HEARING ON CLAIM OF RIGHT TO POSSESSION Hearing Required to address service of pre-judgment claim of right to possession. See Proof of Service filed on February 24, 2020 and item 4 of the instructions on Claim of Right to Possession Form CP-10. Claimant to appear and present evidence of their oral rental agreement with the landlord and claimant's occupancy of the premises as of September 25, 2019. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: CARITAS MANAGEMENT CORPORATION VS. JAMES MCALLISTER ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 29, 2021 line 5. DEFENDANT JAMES MCALISTER'S DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Moving party failed to cite any authority for the proposition that none of the allegations on which the Notice to Quit is based constitutes nuisance as a matter of law. Any dispute as to whether particular allegations amount to nuisance is an issue of fact not appropriate for a determination on a demurrer. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
